response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role is a principal-level hardware performance architecture position. His resume shows no SoC or IP modeling, no AI accelerator or GPU architecture work, no deep-learning workload analysis, and no hardware-software co-design. The closest match is low-latency backend work in DLang, which is software-level performance tuning rather than the silicon/system architecture expertise this role requires.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong testing culture and open-source track record, including C/C++ tooling experience
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator performance background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack IC work, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or DL workload background
